title: Diabetic foot MSK review

examination.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
examination.rationale: Supports clinician-recorded foot-risk assessment domains for diabetic-foot MSK review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

investigations.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
investigations.rationale: Supports entered Dubai diabetes review context for diabetic-foot MSK review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

referral.text: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.
referral.rationale: Supports specialist foot-service and follow-up context for diabetic-foot MSK review; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Diabetes status, ulcer, skin, deformity, sensation, pulses, temperature, infection, footwear, prior amputation, imaging and service plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for diabetic-foot MSK review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
